text: Feature branches are a key part of working with Git. They allow developers to create separate versions of the code to work on new features or fixes without affecting the main project. Once the work is complete, the branch can be merged back into the main branch. This is often done through a pull request, where team members review the changes before they become part of the final code. This workflow helps teams collaborate smoothly, avoid conflicts, and keep the main branch stable. Overall, using branches and pull requests creates a clear and organized version control process.
